history.source_snippet: "We need a software layer like the one AutoGrid offers to link our different assets," says Christophe Tonion. "Even though this market still depends on varied regulations and economic factors such as the cost of storing power, tomorrow's power grids won't be able to function effectively without efficient feed-in and distribution management. Our investment in AutoGrid will enable us to learn a technology and to do so early enough in a market that is still new."

NC_88.current_text: Demand response reduces reliance on fossil fuel power plants by replacing their need

history.text: Demand response reduces reliance on fossil fuel power plants by replacing their need
history.source_snippet: Demand response reduces, or eliminates, the need to resort to gas-, fuel oil- or coal-fired power plants, which can be started up quickly but are bigger polluters and carbon emitters.

history.text: Use of pricing incentives to discourage power consumption during grid stress periods as demand response

NC_89.current_text: Demand response involves asking users to reduce grid power use by shutting down equipment or starting onsite generators

history.text: Demand response involves asking users to reduce grid power use by shutting down equipment or starting onsite generators
history.source_snippet: The usual way to manage demand response is to ask users to draw less from the grid. Customers may be asked to shut down production equipment, for example, or even to start up a generator on site.

history.source_snippet: In industry, a pump, air-conditioning system or battery could be controlled -- shut off and restarted -- in the same way without compromising site efficiency or safety.

history.source_snippet: For example, household heating could be shut off for 10 minutes without significantly interfering with user comfort.

history.source_snippet: By multiplying the number of connected "things" such as smart meters in private homes, economies of scale would ensure balance and demand response in a way virtually unfelt by users -- resulting in lower power bills and a smaller environmental tab.

history.source_snippet: The 450 Scenario sets out an energy pathway consistent with the goal of limiting the global increase in temperature to 2 degreesC by limiting concentration of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere to around 450 parts per million of CO2 .

NC_101.current_text: Reuse of produced water from oil wells reduces freshwater withdrawal and environmental discharge

history.text: Reuse of produced water from oil wells reduces freshwater withdrawal and environmental discharge
history.source_snippet: The water is reused to meet technical requirements at installations, sidestepping the need both to tap the natural water supply and discharge the water produced into the environment.

history.text: Reuse of produced water from oil wells reduces freshwater withdrawal and environmental discharge
history.source_snippet: But it's the water produced along with the oil or gas that is increasingly used today.

history.text: Reuse of produced water from oil wells reduces freshwater withdrawal and environmental discharge
history.source_snippet: Total treats all produced water before reinjecting it into the reservoir.

history.text: Reuse of produced water from oil wells reduces freshwater withdrawal and environmental discharge
history.source_snippet: Both onshore and offshore, the first option people study now is avoiding any discharge into the natural environment and instead injecting produced water back into the reservoir it came from.

NC_102.current_text: Growth of water reinjection practices framed as environmental engagement by oil companies

history.text: Growth of water reinjection practices framed as environmental engagement by oil companies
history.source_snippet: Its future growth, especially in offshore fields, is part of the environmental engagement of oil companies, which are also experiencing mounting regulatory pressures.

history.text: Growth of water reinjection practices framed as environmental engagement by oil companies
history.source_snippet: "So reinjection installations are always tailored to specific needs. 'We start with a standard architecture that we adapt to the specific physicochemical properties of the water to be reinjected.' The main risk is the formation of solid sulfate deposits that are very difficult to remove and can compromise the reinjection equipment. A range of parameters measuring quality, such as bacterial growth, are monitored. 'The higher the water quality desired, the more expensive the installation will be. We have to constantly make trade-offs to strike the right quality, cost and environmental balance,' says Pierre Pedenaud.

history.text: Growth of water reinjection practices framed as environmental engagement by oil companies
history.source_snippet: Produced water reinjection must adapt to each production site's always different environmental, technical and regulatory requirements.

NC_104.current_text: High water content in oil extraction increases over field lifetime

history.text: High water content in oil extraction increases over field lifetime
history.source_snippet: Minimal when a field first comes on stream, it can make up more than 90 percent of the fluids extracted after wells have been in service for 20 or so years.

history.text: High water content in oil extraction increases over field lifetime
history.source_snippet: According to a study by IFP Energies nouvelles (IFPEN), an estimated 250 million barrels of water a day were produced in 2008, a figure expected to jump to 300 million3 in 2020, an increase of 20 percent.

NC_103.current_text: Water is an essential natural element in oil production

history.text: Water is an essential natural element in oil production
history.source_snippet: No oil without water You can't produce oil without water, because water is present naturally in both onshore and offshore oil reservoirs.

history.text: Water is an essential natural element in oil production
history.source_snippet: Consequently, an average of three to five barrels of water1 is produced with one barrel of oil2. That means water is the number one fluid produced by the oil industry.

history.text: Justification of produced water discharge practices as environmentally compliant due to dilution and standards

On offshore platforms, it was mainly discharged into the ocean, taking advantage of a strong dilutive effect while complying with applicable international or local standards4 (generally, less than 30 milligrams of hydrocarbon per liter of water discharged).
